content: In flexibility method the unknown quantities are ----------------- whereas in stiffness method the unknown quantities are --------------
Flexibility of a structure may be defined as the displacement caused by ----------------- force and stiffness of a structure may be defined as the force required for ----------------- displacement
Which of the following statements is true with regard to the flexibility method of analysis.

options.content: The method is used to analyze determine structures.
The method is used only for manual analysis of Indeterminate structures
The method is used for analysis of flexible structures.
The method is used for analysis of indeterminate structures with lesser degree of static indeterminacy.

content: For the frame shown in the figure (not to scale), all members (AB, BC, CD, GB, and CH) have the same length, 𝐿 and flexural rigidity, 𝐸𝐼. The joints at B and C are rigid joints, and the supports A and D are fixed supports. Beams GB and CH carry uniformly distributed loads of 𝑤 per unit length. The magnitude of the moment reaction at A is 𝑤𝐿2⁄𝑘. What is the value of 𝑘 (in integer)? ____________
explanation:
Distribution factors of member BA and BC

$$ \begin{array}{|c|c|c|c|c|} \hline \text { Joint } & \text { Member } & k & \sum k & \begin{array}{l} \text { D.F }=\frac{k}{\sum k} \end{array} \\ \hline \text { B } & \begin{array}{l} \text { BA } \\\\ B C^{\prime} \end{array} & \begin{array}{l} \frac{I}{L} \\ \frac{1}{2}\left(\frac{I}{L}\right) \end{array} & \begin{array}{l} \frac{3}{2}\left(\frac{I}{L}\right) \end{array} & \begin{array}{l} \frac{2}{3} \\ \frac{1}{3} \end{array} \\ \hline \end{array} $$

Distribution of end moment $$ \to $$

From above, moment at A

$$ =\frac{w L^2}{6} $$ .........(i)

Given moment at A

$$ =\frac{w L^2}{k} $$ ...........(ii)

From equation (i) and (ii), $\alpha=6$

Hence, the correct answer is 6 .

content:
Consider the linearly elastic plane frame shown in the figure. Members HF, FK and FG are welded together at joint F. Joints K, G and H are fixed supports. A counter-clockwise moment M is applied at joint F. Consider flexural rigidity El = 105 kN-m2 for each member and neglect axial deformations.

If the magnitude (absolute value) of the support moment at H is 10 kN-m, the magnitude (absolute value) of the applied moment M (in kN-m) to maintain static equilibrium is ___________. (round off to the nearest integer)
explanation:
Given : Flexural rigidity $(E I)=10^5 \mathrm{kN}-\mathrm{m}^2$

Magnitude of the support

Moment at $\mathrm{H}=10 \mathrm{kN}-\mathrm{m}$

The magnitude (absolute value) of the applied moment M is,

$$ \begin{aligned} \frac{M}{6} & =10 \mathrm{kNm} \\\\ M & =6 \times 10 \\\\ M & =60 \mathrm{kNm} \end{aligned} $$

content: Match List - $${\rm I}$$ with List - $${\rm II}$$ and select the correct answer using the codes given below the lists :
Method of Indeterminate structural analysis may be grouped under force method or displacement method. Which of the groupings given below is correct?

options.content:
Force method : Moment distribution method, Consistent deformation method;
Displacement method : Method of three moments, Slope deflection method.

Force method : Method of three moments, Consistent deformation method;
Displacement method : Moment distribution method, Slope deflection method.

Force method : Slope deflection method, Consistent deformation method;
Displacement method : Moment distribution method, Method of three moments.

Force method : Moment distribution method of three moments;
Displacement method : Slope deflection method, Consistent declamation method.
